job_summary:
A Staff Nurse (RN) provides direct patient care, administers medications, monitors patient 
conditions, and coordinates with doctors and healthcare teams to ensure quality treatment.
responsibilities: Assess patient health and record medical history
Monitor vital signs and report changes
Educate patients and families about care plans
Ensure infection control and safety protocols

job_summary:
LPNs provide basic nursing care under the supervision of RNs and doctors, focusing on 
routine patient care and comfort.
qualifications: Diploma in Practical Nursing / GNM equivalent
responsibilities: Assist patients with daily activities (bathing, feeding)
Dress wounds and assist in procedures

job_summary:
A Nursing Officer is a government or institutional role responsible for delivering patient care 
while also handling administrative and supervisory tasks.

A Ward Nurse manages patient care within a specific hospital ward, ensuring smooth day-to
day operations and patient monitoring.
responsibilities: Monitor all patients in the assigned ward
Coordinate with doctors for patient care
Supervise ward assistants and junior staff

job_summary:
A Bedside Nurse provides direct, one-on-one patient care at the patient's bedside, focusing 
on comfort, monitoring, and recovery support.
responsibilities: Assist with feeding, mobility, and hygiene

job_summary:
A Patient Care Nurse focuses on holistic care, ensuring physical, emotional, and 
psychological well-being of patients.
responsibilities: Develop and implement patient care plans
Educate patients on treatment and recovery

job_summary:
A Nurse Practitioner is an advanced practice nurse who can assess, diagnose, and treat 
patients independently or collaboratively with physicians. In many settings, NPs function 
similarly to primary care providers.
qualifications: MSc Nursing / Doctor of Nursing Practice (DNP)
responsibilities: Order and interpret diagnostic tests (lab, imaging)
Provide preventive care and health education

job_summary:
A Clinical Nurse Specialist is an expert in a specialized area of nursing practice (e.g., 
oncology, cardiology). They improve patient outcomes through clinical expertise, education, 
and system-level improvements.
responsibilities: Provide expert consultation on complex cases
Improve quality of care and patient safety
Implement best practices in specialized units

job_summary:
A Certified Registered Nurse Anesthetist (CRNA) administers anesthesia and provides 
perioperative care before, during, and after surgical procedures.
responsibilities: Provide post-anesthesia care and pain management

job_summary:
A Certified Nurse Midwife (CNM) specializes in women's reproductive health, pregnancy, 
childbirth, and postpartum care, often providing holistic and patient-centered maternity 
services.

mandatory: Strong clinical and maternal care skills

responsibilities: Provide prenatal care and health assessments
Provide postpartum care for mother and baby
Offer family planning and reproductive health services
Educate mothers on nutrition, breastfeeding, and newborn care

job_summary:
A Community Health Nurse provides healthcare services to individuals, families, and groups 
within a community, focusing on disease prevention, health promotion, and basic treatment.
responsibilities: Conduct community health assessments and surveys
Provide basic medical care and first aid
Educate the public on hygiene, nutrition, and disease prevention
Monitor outbreaks and report public health issues

job_summary:
A Public Health Nurse works at a broader population level, developing and implementing 
health policies and programs to improve overall public health.
qualifications: BSc Nursing + MPH (Master of Public Health preferred)
responsibilities: Design and implement public health programs
Monitor disease trends and epidemiological data
Work on national programs (TB, HIV, maternal health)

job_summary:
A School Nurse ensures the health and well-being of students and staff within educational 
institutions.
responsibilities: Provide first aid and emergency care
Educate students on hygiene and nutrition

job_summary:
An Occupational Health Nurse focuses on workplace health and safety, preventing work
related injuries and illnesses.
responsibilities: Provide first aid and emergency care

job_summary:
A Home Health Nurse provides personalized medical care to patients in their homes, 
especially those with chronic illness, disability, or post-surgical needs.
responsibilities: Coordinate with doctors for home treatment plans

job_summary:
A Rural Health Nurse provides essential healthcare services in rural or underserved areas 
with limited access to medical facilities.
responsibilities: Conduct immunization and maternal health programs

job_summary:
An ICU Nurse provides intensive, round-the-clock care to critically ill patients who require 
constant monitoring and life-support systems.

mandatory: Ability to work under high pressure

responsibilities: Monitor vital signs using advanced equipment (ECG, ventilators)
Administer IV medications, sedatives, and life-saving drugs
Manage ventilators and other life-support systems
Respond immediately to emergencies (cardiac arrest, organ failure)
Collaborate with intensivists and multidisciplinary teams

job_summary:
An ER Nurse provides immediate care to patients with acute illnesses or injuries in 
emergency departments, often dealing with life-threatening situations.
responsibilities: Perform triage to prioritize patient care
Provide emergency treatment (CPR, wound care, stabilization)
Prepare patients for surgery or admission

job_summary:
A Trauma Nurse specializes in treating patients with severe, life-threatening injuries caused 
by accidents, violence, or disasters.
responsibilities: Coordinate with trauma teams (surgeons, ER staff)

job_summary:
A Critical Care Nurse provides specialized care for patients with life-threatening conditions 
across ICUs, emergency units, and critical care departments.

A Cardiac Nurse specializes in caring for patients with heart-related conditions such as 
coronary artery disease, heart failure, and post-cardiac surgery recovery.
responsibilities: Monitor heart function (ECG, blood pressure, pulse)
Assist in cardiac procedures (angiography, angioplasty, bypass surgery)
Educate patients on lifestyle changes (diet, exercise, smoking cessation)
Recognize and respond to cardiac emergencies

job_summary:
An Oncology Nurse provides care to patients diagnosed with cancer, supporting them 
through diagnosis, treatment, and recovery or palliative care.
responsibilities: Administer chemotherapy, immunotherapy, and targeted treatments
Monitor and manage treatment side effects
Educate patients and families about cancer care
Coordinate with oncologists and multidisciplinary teams
Assist in palliative and end-of-life care

job_summary:
A Neurology Nurse cares for patients with disorders of the brain, spinal cord, and nervous 
system such as stroke, epilepsy, and Parkinson's disease.
responsibilities: Monitor neurological status (consciousness, reflexes, motor function)
Assist in stroke management and rehabilitation
Support patients with mobility and coordination issues
Educate families on long-term neurological care

job_summary:
A Nephrology Nurse specializes in caring for patients with kidney diseases and those 
undergoing dialysis.
responsibilities: Monitor fluid balance and electrolyte levels
Administer medications related to kidney function
Educate patients on diet and lifestyle changes

job_summary:
An Endocrinology Nurse focuses on patients with hormonal disorders such as diabetes, 
thyroid diseases, and metabolic conditions.
responsibilities: Monitor blood glucose levels and hormone-related parameters
Educate patients on insulin therapy and medication use
Assist in managing chronic endocrine conditions

job_summary:
An Operating Room (OR) Nurse, also known as a scrub or circulating nurse, assists surgeons 
during surgical procedures by maintaining a sterile environment and ensuring everything runs 
smoothly in the operating theatre.
responsibilities: Prepare the operating room and surgical instruments
Ensure strict sterile techniques are followed
Assist surgeons during procedures (passing instruments, handling supplies)
Count surgical instruments and materials before and after surgery
Coordinate with the surgical team (anesthetists, technicians)

job_summary:
A Perioperative Nurse provides comprehensive care to surgical patients before, during, and 
after surgery, ensuring patient safety and smooth surgical outcomes.
responsibilities: Conduct pre-operative assessments and prepare patients
Educate patients about surgical procedures and recovery
Assist during surgery (as scrub or circulating nurse)
Ensure continuity of care across all surgical phases

job_summary:
A Surgical Nurse focuses on caring for patients undergoing surgical treatment, primarily in 
pre-operative and post-operative phases.
responsibilities: Prepare patients for surgery (tests, consent, education)
Monitor vital signs and patient condition post-surgery
Detect and manage post-surgical complications (infection, bleeding)
Educate patients on recovery and rehabilitation

job_summary:
A Pediatric Nurse provides medical care to infants, children, and adolescents, focusing on 
growth, development, and treatment of childhood illnesses.
responsibilities: Treat common childhood illnesses and injuries
Assist in pediatric procedures and diagnostics
Educate parents on child health, nutrition, and hygiene
Provide emotional support to children and families

job_summary:
A Neonatal Nurse cares for newborns, especially premature or critically ill infants, in 
Neonatal Intensive Care Units (NICU).
responsibilities: Manage incubators, ventilators, and life-support equipment

job_summary:
An Obstetric Nurse provides care to women during pregnancy (prenatal phase), ensuring the 
health of both mother and fetus.
responsibilities: Monitor fetal development and maternal health
Assist in diagnostic tests (ultrasound support, lab tests)
Educate mothers on pregnancy care and nutrition

job_summary:
A Labor & Delivery Nurse assists women during childbirth, ensuring safe delivery and 
providing emotional and clinical support throughout labor.
responsibilities: Monitor contractions and fetal heart rate
Assist doctors during labor and childbirth
Provide immediate newborn care after birth

job_summary:
A Psychiatric Nurse specializes in caring for patients with mental illnesses and psychiatric 
disorders such as schizophrenia, bipolar disorder, depression, and anxiety. They work 
closely with psychiatrists to provide medical and therapeutic care.
responsibilities: Assess patients' mental health status and behavior
Administer psychiatric medications and monitor side effects
Develop and implement individualized care plans
Assist in psychotherapy and counseling sessions
Maintain detailed patient records and progress notes
Educate patients and families about mental health conditions

job_summary:
A Mental Health Nurse focuses on the emotional, psychological, and behavioral well-being 
of patients, supporting those with stress, anxiety, depression, and other mental health 
challenges.
responsibilities: Provide therapeutic communication and emotional support
Assist in counseling and behavioral therapy programs
Promote mental wellness and preventive care
Work with families and caregivers for holistic care

job_summary:
A Nurse Manager oversees a specific unit or department in a hospital, ensuring high-quality 
patient care while managing nursing staff and daily operations.
responsibilities: Manage duty schedules and staffing levels
Ensure quality patient care and safety standards
Handle patient complaints and resolve issues
Monitor performance and conduct staff evaluations

job_summary:
A Nursing Supervisor oversees multiple nursing units or shifts, ensuring smooth hospital 
operations and proper coordination among departments.
responsibilities: Supervise nursing staff across different departments
Coordinate between doctors, nurses, and administration
Monitor patient care quality across units
Provide guidance and support to staff nurses

job_summary:
The Chief Nursing Officer (CNO) is a top executive responsible for overall nursing services, 
strategy, and leadership within a healthcare organization.
responsibilities: Develop and implement nursing strategies and policies
Oversee all nursing departments and leaders
Ensure quality, safety, and compliance standards
Represent nursing staff at executive level meetings
Drive innovation and improvement in patient care

job_summary:
The Director of Nursing is responsible for managing the entire nursing department, focusing 
on operational efficiency, staff development, and quality care delivery.
responsibilities: Oversee nursing operations across the facility
Develop staffing plans and training programs
Monitor patient care quality and safety

job_summary:
A Hospital Administrator with a nursing background manages overall hospital operations, 
combining clinical knowledge with administrative expertise.
qualifications: BSc Nursing + MBA in Hospital/Healthcare Management (preferred)
responsibilities: Ensure compliance with healthcare laws and standards
Improve patient care services and hospital efficiency
Coordinate between medical, nursing, and administrative teams

job_summary:
A Nurse Educator designs, develops, and delivers educational programs for nursing students 
and practicing nurses, ensuring high standards of clinical knowledge and skills.
responsibilities: Develop nursing curriculum and lesson plans
Teach theoretical and practical nursing subjects
Train nurses in clinical skills and procedures
Conduct workshops, seminars, and training programs
Update teaching methods based on latest healthcare practices

job_summary:
A Clinical Instructor supervises nursing students during their practical training in hospitals, 
ensuring safe and effective patient care practices.
responsibilities: Monitor student performance in real patient settings
Ensure adherence to safety and ethical standards

job_summary:
A Nursing Lecturer teaches nursing subjects at undergraduate and diploma levels, focusing 
on theoretical education and academic development.
responsibilities: Participate in academic planning and curriculum design
Stay updated with current medical advancements

job_summary:
A Professor of Nursing is a senior academic responsible for teaching, research, and 
leadership in nursing education at universities or advanced institutions.

A Nursing Tutor provides foundational education and guidance to nursing students, 
especially at diploma and entry-level programs.
responsibilities: Teach basic nursing subjects and skills

job_summary:
A Nurse Researcher conducts scientific studies to improve patient care, healthcare systems, 
and nursing practices using evidence-based methodologies.
qualifications: PhD in Nursing (preferred for advanced roles)
responsibilities: Collect, analyze, and interpret healthcare data
Apply for research grants and funding

job_summary:
A Clinical Research Nurse manages and participates in clinical trials, ensuring patient safety 
and compliance with research protocols.
qualifications: Training in clinical research (GCP certification preferred)
responsibilities: Recruit and screen participants for clinical trials
Monitor patient responses and side effects
Ensure compliance with ethical and regulatory standards

job_summary:
A Research Coordinator manages the operational aspects of research projects and clinical 
studies, ensuring smooth execution from start to completion.
responsibilities: Communicate with sponsors and research teams

job_summary:
An Evidence-Based Practice (EBP) Specialist ensures that patient care and nursing practices 
are based on the latest scientific evidence and best practices.
qualifications: Training in research and quality improvement
responsibilities: Review and analyze current research studies
Train staff on updated clinical practices
Bridge gap between research and clinical practice

job_summary:
A Nursing Informatics Specialist integrates nursing science with information technology to 
improve patient care, data management, and healthcare systems.
qualifications: BSc Nursing + Certification in Nursing Informatics
responsibilities: Implement and manage Electronic Health Records (EHR) systems
Analyze clinical data to improve patient outcomes
Train healthcare staff on digital systems
Ensure data accuracy, privacy, and security
Collaborate with IT teams to develop healthcare software

job_summary:
A Telehealth Nurse provides remote healthcare services through phone, video calls, or digital 
platforms, improving access to care.
responsibilities: Assess symptoms and provide medical advice
Educate patients on medications and lifestyle
Coordinate care with doctors and specialists

job_summary:
A Clinical Data Analyst with a nursing background analyzes healthcare data to improve 
patient outcomes, hospital performance, and clinical decision-making.
qualifications: Knowledge of tools (Excel, SQL, Python, Power BI – preferred)
responsibilities: Identify trends in patient outcomes and treatments
Collaborate with healthcare and IT teams

job_summary:
A Healthcare AI Trainer uses clinical knowledge to train, validate, and improve artificial 
intelligence models used in healthcare systems.
responsibilities: Annotate and label medical data for AI training
Validate AI outputs for accuracy and safety
Collaborate with AI engineers and developers
Train AI systems on clinical workflows and decision-making
Ensure ethical use of healthcare AI
Improve AI performance using real-world clinical insights

job_summary:
A Medical Advisor (with a nursing background) provides clinical expertise to healthcare 
companies, ensuring that products, services, and strategies are medically accurate and 
effective.
responsibilities: Provide clinical insights for product development and marketing
Support medical content creation and validation
Train sales and marketing teams on medical concepts
Liaise with healthcare professionals and stakeholders
Participate in clinical discussions and advisory boards

job_summary:
A Pharmaceutical Nurse Educator trains healthcare professionals and patients on 
medications, therapies, and medical products.
qualifications: Experience in clinical or pharmaceutical settings
responsibilities: Conduct training sessions on drug usage and safety
Educate healthcare providers about new medications
Provide patient education on treatment plans
Support clinical trials and product launches
Act as a bridge between pharma companies and healthcare providers

job_summary:
An Insurance Nurse works with insurance companies to evaluate medical claims, assess 
patient cases, and ensure appropriate healthcare coverage decisions.
responsibilities: Review medical records and insurance claims
Assess treatment necessity and coverage eligibility
Provide recommendations on claim approvals or denials

job_summary:
A Nurse Case Manager coordinates patient care across different services, ensuring efficient, 
cost-effective, and quality treatment.
responsibilities: Develop and manage patient care plans
Coordinate between doctors, hospitals, and insurers
Educate patients on treatment and recovery

job_summary:
A Healthcare Consultant with a nursing background advises healthcare organizations on 
improving operations, patient care, and overall efficiency.
qualifications: Experience in clinical or administrative roles
responsibilities: Develop strategies to enhance patient care and efficiency
Advise on healthcare policies and regulations

job_summary:
A Forensic Nurse provides medical care to victims of crime while also collecting and 
preserving evidence for legal investigations.
responsibilities: Examine victims of assault, abuse, or violence
Collect forensic evidence (DNA samples, injury documentation)
Maintain chain of custody for legal evidence
Provide medical treatment and emotional support
Testify in court as an expert witness (in some cases)
Collaborate with law enforcement and legal authorities

job_summary:
A Legal Nurse Consultant assists lawyers and legal teams by interpreting medical records and 
providing expert insights in medical-related legal cases.
responsibilities: Identify standards of care and possible negligence
Prepare medical reports for legal cases
Assist attorneys in understanding medical terminology
Provide expert opinions in court cases

job_summary:
A Nurse Auditor reviews healthcare records and processes to ensure compliance with medical 
standards, billing accuracy, and regulatory requirements.
responsibilities: Audit patient records and treatment documentation
Ensure compliance with healthcare laws and policies

job_summary:
A Medical Claims Reviewer evaluates healthcare claims to ensure treatments are necessary, 
appropriate, and covered under insurance policies.
qualifications: Experience in clinical or insurance sector
responsibilities: Review medical claims and patient records
Identify fraud or misuse of healthcare services
Communicate with healthcare providers and insurers

job_summary:
A Travel Nurse works on short-term assignments in different hospitals or regions, often 
filling staffing shortages.
qualifications: Valid nursing license (local/international like NCLEX for abroad)
responsibilities: Provide direct patient care in assigned facilities
Adapt quickly to new clinical environments
Assist during staff shortages or peak demand

job_summary:
A Cruise Ship Nurse provides medical care to passengers and crew aboard ships, handling 
everything from minor illnesses to emergencies.
responsibilities: Provide emergency and primary care onboard
Respond to medical emergencies at sea
Conduct health screenings and infection control

job_summary:
A Military Nurse serves in the armed forces, providing care to soldiers, veterans, and 
sometimes civilians in military or field settings.
responsibilities: Deliver medical care in hospitals or field units
Assist in surgeries and trauma care
Participate in military training and missions

job_summary:
A Disaster Relief Nurse provides emergency healthcare during natural disasters, 
pandemics, or crises.
responsibilities: Deliver urgent care in disaster zones

job_summary:
A Humanitarian Nurse works with NGOs to provide healthcare services in underserved, 
rural, or conflict-affected areas.
responsibilities: Manage maternal and child health services
Support vaccination drives and disease prevention

job_summary:
A Palliative Care Nurse focuses on improving the quality of life for patients with serious or 
terminal illnesses by managing symptoms and providing emotional support.
qualifications: Training in palliative or hospice care (preferred)
responsibilities: Manage pain and other distressing symptoms
Educate families on disease progression and care

job_summary:
A Pain Management Nurse specializes in assessing and treating acute and chronic pain 
using medications and therapeutic interventions.
responsibilities: Administer pain relief medications and therapies
Educate patients on pain control strategies

job_summary:
An Infection Control Nurse works to prevent and control infections within healthcare 
settings, ensuring patient and staff safety.

mandatory: Knowledge of microbiology and infection control

responsibilities: Train staff on hygiene and safety practices

job_summary:
A Dialysis Nurse cares for patients with kidney failure, performing dialysis treatments and 
monitoring patient response.

A Transplant Nurse manages care for patients undergoing organ transplants, from pre
surgery preparation to post-operative recovery.
qualifications: Experience in critical care or transplant units
responsibilities: Educate patients on medication and lifestyle changes

job_summary:
A Cosmetic Nurse provides beauty and aesthetic treatments such as Botox, fillers, and skin 
therapies.
responsibilities: Educate clients on skincare and aftercare

job_summary:
A Rehabilitation Nurse helps patients recover from injuries, surgeries, or disabilities, 
focusing on restoring function and independence.

A Geriatric Nurse specializes in caring for elderly patients, managing age-related health 
conditions and promoting quality of life.

A Nursing Assistant provides basic patient care and support under the supervision of 
registered nurses and doctors.
responsibilities: Assist patients with daily activities (bathing, dressing, feeding)
Monitor vital signs (temperature, pulse, blood pressure)
Help patients move, turn, or walk
Maintain cleanliness and hygiene of patients

job_summary:
A Patient Care Technician provides advanced support to nurses, performing both basic care 
and some clinical tasks.
responsibilities: Assist with patient care (hygiene, mobility, feeding)
Perform basic clinical tasks (ECG, specimen collection)

job_summary:
A Health Care Assistant supports nurses and healthcare professionals in delivering day-to
day patient care, especially in long-term and community settings.
responsibilities: Assist with personal care and hygiene
Help patients with mobility and daily activities
Monitor patient conditions and report changes

job_summary:
A Phlebotomy Nurse specializes in drawing blood samples for diagnostic tests, transfusions, 
or donations.
qualifications: BSc Nursing / GNM or Phlebotomy certification
responsibilities: Collect blood samples safely and accurately

job_summary:
An Addiction Nurse specializes in caring for patients struggling with substance abuse and 
addiction (alcohol, drugs, prescription misuse), supporting detoxification, recovery, and 
relapse prevention.
qualifications: Training or certification in addiction/psychiatric nursing (preferred)
responsibilities: Assess patients for substance use disorders
Monitor patients during detox and withdrawal
Administer medications for addiction treatment (e.g., withdrawal management)
Educate patients and families about addiction and recovery
Collaborate with psychiatrists, therapists, and rehab teams

job_summary:
A Behavioral Health Nurse focuses on behavioral patterns, emotional disorders, and 
mental health conditions, helping patients manage behaviors affecting their well-being.
responsibilities: Develop and implement behavioral care plans
Assist in therapy sessions (CBT, behavioral therapy)
Support patients in developing healthy coping mechanisms
Work with families for holistic care

job_summary:
A Counseling Nurse provides guidance, emotional support, and therapeutic 
communication to patients dealing with stress, illness, trauma, or life challenges.
responsibilities: Conduct counseling sessions for patients and families
Help patients cope with illness, stress, or trauma
Provide mental health education and awareness
Refer patients to specialists when needed

job_summary:
A Government Staff Nurse provides clinical care in public healthcare facilities, ensuring 
accessible and affordable treatment to the community.
responsibilities: Deliver direct patient care (medication, monitoring, procedures)
Assist doctors in diagnosis and treatment
Participate in public health programs (immunization, maternal care)
Follow government healthcare protocols and standards
Educate patients on health and hygiene

job_summary:
A Health Inspector (with a nursing background) ensures public health safety and sanitation 
standards in communities.
qualifications: Diploma/Certification in Public Health or Sanitary Inspection
responsibilities: Inspect sanitation, hygiene, and environmental conditions
Monitor food safety and water quality
Enforce public health laws and regulations
Report health hazards and recommend corrective actions

job_summary:
A Healthcare Policy Advisor develops and evaluates health policies and programs to 
improve healthcare systems at regional or national levels.
qualifications: BSc Nursing + MPH / MHA / Public Policy (preferred)
Experience in public health or administration
responsibilities: Collaborate with government bodies and stakeholders
Draft reports, guidelines, and policy documents

job_summary:
A Program Manager oversees public health programs, ensuring effective planning, 
implementation, and evaluation.
responsibilities: Plan and manage healthcare programs (e.g., vaccination, maternal health)
Coordinate with healthcare teams and stakeholders
